mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate earnings capacity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as earnings estimates are revised upward.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year IC of GDPC1 level against forward 60-day SPX returns is below 0.05, or the signal is negative in at least 60% of periods with GDP growth above trend.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate from trough levels breaks the narrative of a tight labor market, raising recession fears and reducing risk appetite, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: In instances where the trigger is activated, the 20-day forward SPX return is positive more than 50% of the time, or the average return is not significantly negative at the 95% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: When both consumer price inflation and the federal funds rate rise concurrently, the central bank is actively tightening policy to combat inflation, which raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, producing negative forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS month-over-month change > 0
failure_condition: The interaction term (product of standardized changes) has a positive coefficient in a linear regression against forward 60-day returns, or the signal fails to outperform a simple single-variable model.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime of low and stable consumer price inflation, rising industrial production signals real economic growth that directly increases corporate earnings without triggering monetary tightening, thus positively driving S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: In periods where CPI year-over-year is below 3%, the IC of INDPRO level against 20-day forward returns is less than 0.05, or the signal is negative in more than half of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: New housing starts represent forward-looking investment in residential construction, which has strong multiplier effects on the economy and correlates with consumer confidence, making rising starts a positive signal for S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The Spearman rank correlation between HOUST level and 20-day forward SPX returns is less than 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises to a level not seen in the prior six months, the market reprices equities to reflect tighter financial conditions, leading to a negative forward return over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS exceeds its 6-month moving average by more than 25 basis points
failure_condition: After trigger activation, the 20-day forward SPX return is positive in more than 50% of occurrences, or the average return is not significantly negative at the 90% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: When job growth is robust and the unemployment rate falls simultaneously, labor market strength raises disposable income and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week as earnings expectations improve.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change > 150,000 and UNRATE month-over-month change < 0
failure_condition: The joint signal (both conditions met) produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction coefficient in a regression is not statistically significant at the 10% level.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high real GDP growth, moderate increases in consumer prices signal robust demand and allow firms to pass through costs, enhancing profit margins and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
regime_hint: high GDP growth (quarter-over-quarter annualized real GDP growth > 3%)
failure_condition: In periods where real GDP growth exceeds 3% annualized, the correlation between CPIAUCSL level and forward 60-day returns is negative, or the average return when CPIAUCSL rises is below zero.
